title: Czech Republic at the 2026 World Cup: Squad, Key Players and Everything You Need to Know
original_text:
The Czech Republic returns to the 2026 FIFA World Cup after a 20-year absence , aiming to reconnect with the competitive legacy built under Czechoslovakia . The European side enters the global stage with a clear identity: tactical discipline, resilience and efficiency in key moments.

Drawn into Group A alongside Mexico, South Korea and South Africa , the Czech team may not be favorites, but they are a dangerous opponent capable of competing through structure and game intelligence.

The Czech Republic qualified through the UEFA playoffs in a campaign defined by resilience. They overturned a 2-0 deficit against Ireland to reach penalties in the semifinals, then defeated Denmark in another dramatic shootout after a 2-2 draw.

Rather than dominance, it was a display of competitive resistance: the team survived difficult moments and executed with precision when it mattered most.

While the Czech Republic ’s standalone World Cup history is limited, its legacy as Czechoslovakia is significant. The nation finished runner-up twice and won the 1976 European Championship.

That team became iconic for its tactical intelligence and composure under pressure, symbolized by the famous penalty by Antonín Panenka .

Since the country’s split, the Czech Republic has experienced inconsistency, making this return to the World Cup a culmination of a long rebuilding process.

The team is led by Miroslav Koubek , who at the age of 75 has built a side with a clear philosophy: defend, endure and strike at the right moment.

His approach prioritizes structure and efficiency over possession, a strategy that proved decisive in qualification.

All eyes are on Patrik Schick , the Bayer Leverkusen striker and the team’s main attacking threat.

His ability to convert limited chances makes him essential in a system that values efficiency over volume.

The Czech Republic enters the tournament aiming to advance past the group stage, something they failed to achieve in their only appearance as an independent nation.

Beyond results, their goal is to establish a clear identity and prove that their return is not temporary, but the beginning of a new competitive era.

Follow all the action from the 2026 FIFA World Cup on beIN SPORTS.
